content:
1. Adjunct Compensation & Workload Expectations
Unlike most colleges, where adjuncts are paid a set amount per course, St. Paul’s requires hourly time-tracking, meaning work outside of classroom hours isn’t automatically factored in. Traditionally, adjunct pay accounts for grading, student engagement, course development, and prep work as part of the contract. The hourly model here suggests that only in-class time is compensated, which raises some questions about workload expectations:
 
·         Should time spent grading and providing feedback be logged for compensation, or is it considered unpaid work?
·         Should course design, student correspondence, and required curriculum work only be completed on clocked-in hours, or is there a different standard for adjuncts?
 
  1. Breakdown of Additional Hours Logged Beyond Classroom Instruction
  2. Canvas Course Redesign, Grading & Administrative Work
